channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Crooks are hijacking and reselling AI infrastructure: Report
item.description: For years, CSOs have worried about their IT infrastructure being used for unauthorized cryptomining. Now, say researchers, they’d better start worrying about crooks hijacking and reselling access to exposed corporate AI infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Audi declares war on ‘giant iPad’ dashboards – as design boss signals return to the analog buttons everyone’s craving
item.description: Audi's design boss sees a return to analog switches and dials for the brand, as massive screens aren't the answer.
item.dc:creator: Leon Poultney, TechRadar

item.title: Tiny autonomous robots can now swim on their own
item.description: Scientists create smallest programmable robots ever – smaller than salt grains, these breakthrough microscopic machines could revolutionize medicine and healthcare.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Microsoft and Meta earnings put a focus on payoffs from AI spending
item.description: Investors wondering about payoffs from the pile of cash being spent on artificial intelligence should get some clues when Microsoft Corp. and Meta Platforms Inc. post earnings after the close Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Big Tech needs less than a month to pay off over $7 billion in 2025 fines, Proton warns
item.description: Big Tech was slapped with $7.8 billion in fines in 2025, but Proton found this massive sum represents less than a month of free cash flow.
item.dc:creator: Rene Millman, TechRadar

item.title: The UK government gets it spectacularly wrong on AI – just 3% of the public agree with its stance on copyright law changes
item.description: A major public consultation revealed overwhelming opposition to the UK government's AI copyright proposal
item.dc:creator: Eric Hal Schwartz, TechRadar

item.title: Gemini Flash model gets visual reasoning capability
item.description: Google has added an Agentic Vision capability to its Gemini 3 Flash model, which the company said combines visual reasoning with code execution to ground answers in visual evidence. The capability fundamentally changes how AI models process images, according to Google.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: France just approved a social media ban for kids under 15
item.description: French lawmakers approved a bill banning social media for children under 15, paving the way for the measure to enter into force at the start of the next school year in September, as the idea of setting a minimum age for use of the platforms gains momentum across Europe.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Anthropic cofounder Daniela Amodei says trusted enterprise AI will transcend the hype cycle
item.description: While Silicon Valley argues over bubbles, benchmarks, and who has the smartest model, Anthropic has been focused on solving problems that rarely generate hype but ultimately determine adoption: whether AI can be trusted to operate inside the world’s most sensitive systems.
item.dc:creator: Victor Dey, Fast Company

item.title: How the Grammys are adapting in a world of AI music, ‘KPop Demon Hunters,’ and a changing music marketplace
item.description: The Grammy Awards return February 1 at a pivotal moment for the music industry, one shaped by trending Latin artists, resurgent rock legends, and even charting AI acts. To unpack what will make this year’s broadcast distinctive, the Recording Academy CEO Harvey Mason Jr.
item.dc:creator: Robert Safian, Fast Company

item.title: 2026 network outage report and internet health check
item.description: ThousandEyes, a Cisco company, monitors how ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services are handling any performance challenges and provides Network World with a weekly roundup of events that impact service delivery.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Alibaba’s Qwen3-Max-Thinking expands enterprise AI model choices
item.description: Alibaba Cloud’s latest AI model, Qwen3-Max-Thinking, is staking a claim as one of the world’s most advanced reasoning engines after posting benchmark results that delivered competitive results against leading models from Google and OpenAI.
item.dc:creator: Info World
